name: Downeast Blueberry Bread Pudding
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Sugar (or use an equivalent amount of sweetener)
1 pint Fresh blueberries, sorted and stemmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sugar, cornstarch, and sweetener in a bowl, pressing out all lumps.
2. Add the blueberries to a 6-cup shallow casserole that has been lightly sprayed with nonstick vegetable cooking spray.
3. Pour in the sugar mixture, add the lemon juice, and toss well to mix.
4. Using a potato masher, lightly mash the blueberries. Set aside.
5. Butter one side of the bread slices (using margarine), stack them, and cut into 1/2-inch cubes.
6. Add the cubed bread to the casserole and toss well to mix.
7. Bake the pudding uncovered in a moderate 350°F oven for 30 minutes.
8. Remove from the oven, add the evaporated skim milk, and stir well to mix.
9. Return to the oven and bake uncovered for an additional 15 minutes.
10. Serve warm, topped with a little whole milk if desired. Makes 6 servings.
